### 816. 6991044160317235200

- URL: https://www.linkedin.com/posts/outboundphd_something-crazy-happens-when-we-sit-down-activity-6991044160317235200-Sj3U
- Author: 🦾Eric Nowoslawski (@outboundphd)
- Posted: 2022-10-26T14:33:35.139Z

#### Verbatim Content

Something crazy happens when we sit down and start writing our outbound emails. 

We say things that we would never say. 

"Just checking in"
"Following up" 
"Book a time on my calendly"

ME ME ME

Try this when starting your next campaign. 

Think about that email you wrote when reaching out to your favorite influencer, author, podcaster, or mentor. How do you speak to them?

Of course, you have a ton of context to go off of and make things very relevant but notice what else you do. You speak to them like a normal human. 

If you do that, you'll save yourself thousands in buying email copywriting courses by just going back to speaking like a human.

### 817. 6989313836453371904

- URL: https://www.linkedin.com/posts/outboundphd_companies-will-charge-you-big-just-to-activity-6989313836453371904-fyth
- Author: 🦾Eric Nowoslawski (@outboundphd)
- Posted: 2022-10-21T19:57:53.782Z

#### Verbatim Content

Companies will charge you big $$$$ just to update your database for people that have changed jobs. Here's the easiest way to do this that doesn't cost your first born. (Looking at someone that rhymes with smoother REMs)

Pull a list from your CRM with whatever data you have. First Name, Last Name, Company name is all you need but emails never hurt! 

Use the Clay LinkedIn Integration to find those people on LinkedIn. The output will tell you where they are currently working AND their current work email address. 

Export and you're good to go. 

Stop over paying for data everyone is just getting from LinkedIn.

### 818. 6992877873552318464

- URL: https://www.linkedin.com/posts/outboundphd_email-delivery-gets-way-easier-when-your-activity-6992877873552318464-Olg0
- Author: 🦾Eric Nowoslawski (@outboundphd)
- Posted: 2022-10-31T16:00:06.443Z

#### Verbatim Content

Email delivery gets way easier when your offer resonates with your list.  
 
Last week, I was asked what matters most when sending a cold email campaign in order to stay out of spam.  
 
Immediately I said, "well, stay our of spam..." 
 
It seems so easy but as people go down the email delivery rabbit hole, they forget why they event went down.  
 
If no one is marking you as spam, you could be sending 2000 emails per day from one Gsuite inbox and go nuts.  
 
But no offer is going to resonate with 100% of the people you message which is why steps need to be taken to protect your delivery and your domain reputation.  
 
But there's no way to save a campaign constantly being marked as spam.  
 
Your offer is more important than everything. If things aren't working, start there every time.

### 819. 6988555730429575170

- URL: https://www.linkedin.com/posts/outboundphd_trigger-events-to-use-in-your-prospecting-activity-6988555730429575170-4qn7
- Author: 🦾Eric Nowoslawski (@outboundphd)
- Posted: 2022-10-19T17:45:27.221Z

#### Verbatim Content

Trigger events to use in your prospecting and where to get the data...let me know if I missed any 👇👇👇

Oldie but still a goodie - New Fundraise announcement -- Crunchbase, Apollo.io, are great bangs for their buck. (Try not to do what everyone else is doing and congratulate them on the raise though). 

New CXO Hire - Save you accounts in Sales Nav and Look for new CXO additions to the company in your department. Automate this with TexAu ⚡ and enrich with Clay. 

Installed a competitor 6-9 months ago. Look for companies that have installed our competitor and they could be coming up on a buying window. BuiltWith has all this information for you!

Hiring for a role that your product or service replaces or enhances. Jordan Crawford has the goods on this. 

Companies that have seen an increase in headcount - Sales Nav will give you the past 12 months increase. Harmonic Labs will tell you the past 30, 60, 90 days. 

Social Media growth - great for finding companies that have traction and may be looking to grow but aren't hiring anyone right now. Again Harmonic Labs has great data on this. 

Companies that have announced layoffs - check layoffs.fyi

Companies that announce a new partnership. Monitor your ICP with Clay and the Google Search Integration to always check on this

Check for companies in the news - LinkedIn is ok with this but this can be automated with Clay as well. 

Shares an office with one of your customers. Apollo.io and a nice Index Match in Google Sheets can help you with this. 

One of your past customer contacts changes jobs. Automate this with Sales Nav, Phantombuster and Clay. 

One of your past naysayers leaves their job! Same as above. 

I was just riffing here, any other triggers that you use in your prospecting?

### 820. 6986359841392893952

- URL: https://www.linkedin.com/posts/outboundphd_sales-connection-jointhekiln-activity-6986359841392893952-2oDa
- Author: 🦾Eric Nowoslawski (@outboundphd)
- Posted: 2022-10-13T16:19:46.477Z

#### Verbatim Content

My favorite relationship building hack right now...

Everyone talks a big talk about how "relationships are all about giving value," "lead with value" "value first." But how do you actually do it?

Right now, here's my favorite way of providing value to cold connections up front. I take the people that have liked their recent posts and turn it into a list for them and highlight what I think would be their ideal customers. 

The message looks like this. 

"Hey {{First Name}}, I noticed that post you did awhile back got some great traction. I found everyone that liked your post and their email address. 

I filtered what I think are probably great leads to you at the top. 

If you ever want to chat about great things to say to these people, I would be glad to connect."

And then I give them the link. No Gatekeeping, no opt in, no attribution tracking. Just a straight value bomb. 

It's ridiculously easy to get this information with tools like Clay, or TexAu ⚡

Just comment or DM and I can show you how. #sales #connection #jointhekiln

### 821. 6980964775077761024

- URL: https://www.linkedin.com/posts/outboundphd_sales-prospecting-outbound-activity-6980964775077761024-_Nqf
- Author: 🦾Eric Nowoslawski (@outboundphd)
- Posted: 2022-09-28T19:01:42.432Z

#### Verbatim Content

Let’s go over how you can automate congratulating your first level connections on LinkedIn when they change jobs and organize this in your CRM.

All you need is Sales Navigator, Phantombuster, and Clay.

Step 1) Create a Sales Navigator List with just two filters. Add your first level connections and add the “Changed Jobs in the last 90 days” filter. Save this search so you can reference it again.

Step 2) Go to your Phantombuster account and create a Sales Navigator Search extractor phantom. Connect your account and add the search URL that we just created. Make sure you put this phantom on “Watcher mode” so that when it runs in the future, no duplicates are pulled.

Run this search so that we have data to populate our Clay table with.

Step 3) Once the search is run, we can start a new table from a source in Clay. Authenticate your Phantombuster account and pull the search into your table.

Note that the LinkedIn profile URL extracted from this search is basically worthless. Use the “Enrich Person From Search” LinkedIn Integration to find their default URL.

Step 4) Enrich the LinkedIn Company so that we have a domain and you can add the Clay “Find Work Email” Integration to pull their new email address.
Now you are ready to enrich this data with anything you want. 

You have their new company website, their LinkedIn profile URL, their company URL, etc. 

Everything you need to start enriching for your workflow.

Step 5) Push your data anywhere. I ended this workflow by updating my CRM but you can also use the Phantombuster Integration to send them a message on LinkedIn as well.

With this workflow, you should never miss another job change and you’ll look like a stud with your first level connections.

I will leave the loom video with the full workflow in the comments!

Happy Hunting!

#sales #prospecting #outbound

### 822. 6983877323594829824

- URL: https://www.linkedin.com/posts/outboundphd_bold-prediction-cold-email-as-an-regular-activity-6983877323594829824-5hye
- Author: 🦾Eric Nowoslawski (@outboundphd)
- Posted: 2022-10-06T19:55:08.111Z

#### Verbatim Content

Bold prediction: Cold email as an regular outbound channel is going to become even harder in the next 6 months. What's happening and what are we going to do about it?

Threats

Spam folders are increasingly getting smarter and more effective at blocking promotional content. 

Sales Engagement platforms are making it continuously easier and cheaper to launch cold email campaigns. Hence creating a further flywheel for more robust spam filtering. 

Companies like Gated are keeping unsolicited mail completely out of the inbox (this is also an opportunity in disguise).

Prospects are increasingly becoming more frustrated with the amount of unsolicited mail they receive and are marking more emails as spam (again, creating a flywheel for more robust spam filtering).

Email sending platforms (Google/Outlook/Zoho) are becoming stricter about how much unsolicited mail they will tolerate in their systems. 

Opportunities

More interesting data sources are becoming increasingly easy to purchase or get your hands on. These data sources help refine your Ideal Customer Profile down to a more perfect fit ICP. Think turning a lake into a pond. 

Tools like Smartlead are helping prospectors escape the spam filter with a robust warm up pool and multi inbox sending powered by Vaibhav 

Apollo.io continues to add robust filtering straight into the platform with a crazy amount of data coverage. They just released intent data straight into the product as well. See Jennifer Rhima for more. 

And of course Clay continues to ship new integrations so that you can build your own databases. Use their Google Integration to find anything about a company you would usually be looking for. 

What would you add to the list?

cc: Jordan Crawford Jesse Ouellette Jared Robin 🔥 Tom Slocum Amelia Taylor

### 824. 6985961775015829504

- URL: https://www.linkedin.com/posts/outboundphd_if-you-had-to-guess-how-buyers-want-to-be-activity-6985961775015829504-GsP_
- Author: 🦾Eric Nowoslawski (@outboundphd)
- Posted: 2022-10-12T13:58:00.057Z

#### Verbatim Content

If you had to guess how buyers want to be contacted by sellers, what would be your guess? Did you think it was email? 

In the 2022 Global State of Sales Report by LinkedIn, 66% of respondents said email was the way that they prefer to be contacted. 

This is exciting data to share but remember, the bar to send a good cold email increases every day. 

You must have all of your technical set ups locked in (SPF, DKIM, DMARC). 

You must have your list cleaned and verified filled with customers that you can actually help. 

You must be bringing value. Show them something that you have learned about their market or a unique insight they don't have because they don't do what you do. 

Keep those emails under 100 words and follow some of the best in the game below for more content about how to prospect in 2022.

Jordan Crawford Amelia Taylor Jesse Ouellette Cody James Justin Michael Patrick Manfra Jason Hubbard

### 826. 6979129071146299392

- URL: https://www.linkedin.com/posts/outboundphd_jointhekiln-activity-6979129071146299392-zZSs
- Author: 🦾Eric Nowoslawski (@outboundphd)
- Posted: 2022-09-23T17:27:16.509Z

#### Verbatim Content

There’s over 30k lawyers in New Jersey. Except you only want to connect with ones that handle wrongful termination. How do you score the list? 

Do you DIY manually? Hire a Virtual Assistant? Microworkers? 

All of these could work, but Clay’s Google integration makes this pretty easy. 

We first start with a list of accounts and their websites.
Add the Google integration and use the search operator site:{{website}} “wrongful termination”. 

With this operator, we are telling Google we want to scan the website and only give us a result back if this website has the words “wrongful termination” mentioned anywhere.

The results will turn your ocean of Lawyers into a pond that’s manageable and highly targeted.

Check out the video below on how you can do it yourself:

#jointhekiln

### 827. 6984195365981855744

- URL: https://www.linkedin.com/posts/outboundphd_if-i-had-to-teach-someone-cold-email-copywriting-activity-6984195365981855744-e8ls
- Author: 🦾Eric Nowoslawski (@outboundphd)
- Posted: 2022-10-07T16:58:55.325Z

#### Verbatim Content

If I had to teach someone cold email copywriting, here's the checklist I would give them. I'm putting this out here because I'm definitely missing bullets and anyone can chime in here. 

1. Keep the message short. No one is going to read a wall of text.
2. Tell them why you are reaching out, how you can help, who else have you helped before.
3. Keep your call to action to gauge interest. "book a call on my calendly" is not the way. I much more prefer, "If we could help you solve this problem, would that be useful to you?"
4. Keep your subject line short and punchy but don't give away the whole email.
5. Don't use traditional break up emails. "I've emailed you 5 times and you haven't responded so I'm letting you know I'm stopping." Who even came up with this?
6. Don't put them on a 17 email long sequence. 
7. Lead with value. What can you tell them about their market, customers, or industry that would actually add value to their lives or business. 

What else would you add? 

cc: the best copywriter in the game Patrick William Joyce

### 828. 6980240683886338048

- URL: https://www.linkedin.com/posts/outboundphd_dont-attend-another-event-without-coming-activity-6980240683886338048-fbn9
- Author: 🦾Eric Nowoslawski (@outboundphd)
- Posted: 2022-09-26T19:04:25.639Z

#### Verbatim Content

Don’t attend another event without coming in prepared. 

The most common scraping request I get is when people are attending an event, they want to reach out to the speakers and sponsors. 

Clay makes this remarkably easy.

In three steps, you can…

-Collect your data
-Find their LinkedIn profiles
-Find their email address

And start networking with the speakers in 5 minutes. 

Essentially, we used the Clay data scraper to collect the data publicly available on the Web Summit page. 

From there, we pushed the data to a Clay table where we enriched the names and company names into LinkedIn profiles and found the Speakers work email address. 

End to end, the whole process took less than 4 minutes and the emails are ready to be uploaded to your campaign. 

Don’t forget to add some relevant personalization and context for your outreach!

### 829. 6973671312523194368

- URL: https://www.linkedin.com/posts/outboundphd_if-joining-communities-is-your-thing-i-just-activity-6973671312523194368-q_UP
- Author: 🦾Eric Nowoslawski (@outboundphd)

### 831. 6978861966207369216

- URL: https://www.linkedin.com/posts/outboundphd_jointhekiln-activity-6978861966207369216-xxLm
- Author: 🦾Eric Nowoslawski (@outboundphd)
- Posted: 2022-09-22T23:45:53.729Z

#### Verbatim Content

There’s a much better way to reach out to companies that recently raised money. 

Companies that have recently raised money is a great trigger to reach out to someone, but often the message falls flat on its face.

Most fundraising outreaches look like this to the buyer.

“Hey {{First Name}}, I noticed you recently raised a lot of money! I like money, do you want to spend your money with me?” 

In Clay, you can easily score a list of target accounts by criteria you would often manually be looking up. 

I had the opportunity to speak with Rohan Suri at Nooks recently and he had a great use case for this. 

Nooks is a virtual sales floor for remote teams (and they just launched a power dialer!). Their best customers 1) Have an SDR Team. 2) Work remotely. 

Instead of looking up each of these companies individually, I scored a list of over 3,000 companies by SDR team headcount and if they work remote. 

You can see in the video below.

The TL;DR is that I used the “Get Counts of Roles by Company” to find all of the companies with SDRs and BDRs. Then I used a combination of Google searches to find if the team works remote.

#jointhekiln

### 833. 6972946601610768385

- URL: https://www.linkedin.com/posts/outboundphd_no-code-is-not-the-next-big-thing-its-already-activity-6972946601610768385-QHDN
- Author: 🦾Eric Nowoslawski (@outboundphd)
- Posted: 2022-09-06T16:00:20.907Z

#### Verbatim Content

No code is NOT the next big thing. It's already here...and you're missing out. Here are 5 of my favorite No code tools right now. 

1) Clay

You may not have heard of this one yet, but you're about to be all over it. 

Build your lead lists, enrich your CRM, score your TAM all in one platform. 

I use it to add wild personalizations to my email campaigns and to find new leads automatically. 

2) Notion

Is any no code list complete without Notion or Airtable (coming up next). What I love about Notion is that the project management functions seemlessly integrate with anything you could want to create Standard Operating Procedures.

I had the opportunity to chat about what others are doing with Notion at Collision conference with Madhu Muthukumar, big things keep on coming!

3) Airtable

Another amazing tool that can bring your databases to life. Love using this as a dumping ground for data because it integrates so well with everything. 

You can also build better CRMs than most CRMs on Airtable and the Softr.io folks are doing amazing things to take Airtable to the next level. 

4) TexAu ⚡

Amazing tool for those that have no clue how to scrape data. Speed up your workflow of scraping multiple websites with TexAu with zero experience required.

5) SmartLead

This is one of my favorite tools to send cold emails because it does not charge users per inbox and it has everything you need from email warm up, spintax, and inbox rotation. And it keeps getting better every week. The roadmap is looking amazing. 

What else should I add to the list?

### 834. 6972221775971549184

- URL: https://www.linkedin.com/posts/outboundphd_ask-better-questions-get-better-answers-activity-6972221775971549184-nd1C
- Author: 🦾Eric Nowoslawski (@outboundphd)
- Posted: 2022-09-04T16:00:09.008Z

#### Verbatim Content

Ask better questions, get better answers.

This is a common theme that comes up when Jordan Crawford and I discuss outbound and marketing. 

Most companies are still thinking about marketing with a 2015 tech stack in mind. 

Most of the data of your perfect customer is out there. You just have to find the developers that are scraping for it. 

Some of the coolest databases I've come across are boutique projects made by developers trying to quit their day jobs.

Great places to find these are rapidapi(dot)com and apify(dot)com.

Take a look at the data available there and let your brain open up to the possibilities.

### 836. 6980612178886193152

- URL: https://www.linkedin.com/posts/outboundphd_does-your-email-personalization-end-at-activity-6980612178886193152-x-bF
- Author: 🦾Eric Nowoslawski (@outboundphd)
- Posted: 2022-09-27T19:40:36.949Z

#### Verbatim Content

Does your email personalization end at {{First Name}}?

The bar for a successful cold email gets raised every day. As Outreach and SalesLoft onboard more customers, standing out becomes more difficult. Here are 5 personalization ideas that can be created completely automatically that give your outreach more spice.

Some aim to create highly relevant messaging for your prospects. Others just help question whether or not the email was fully automated or not. 

1) Add how long the person has held the role into your outreach.

"Hey Jared Robin, noticed you've been the Co-Founder of RevGenius since June 2020 so I figured you would be the best person to reach out to."

2) Add how many roles the person held before their current one.

P.S. Vaibhav Namburi, noticed you held 21 jobs before you launched Five2One. If we ever connect, I'd like to know which one was the best experience that inspired Five2One.

3) Pull in technology from their website (points for pulling multiple that integrate with yours).

Hey Rohan Suri, noticed Nooks is using Leadfeeder when I pulled up the Builtwith Chrome extension. These tools are usually using their own proprietary data sets and adding another is going to uncover even more site visitors for you. Can I intro you to my friend Jesse Ouellette who runs a similar product that has been able to identify more leads than Leadfeeder for his users?

4) Mention an audit you did of their competitors

Hey Elric Legloire, noticed Calendly just saw a bump in traffic through paid ads and they recently added a TikTok ads pixel. If I made a video of their new strategy, would you want to see it?

5) Mention local facts or hotspots to get their attention. I like to mention the current weather, the top restaurant in the area, or the top attraction.

P.S. Rocco Savage, noticed you're based in Miami, have you ever tried KYU? I've heard amazing things about it!

Keep in mind a RELEVANT email is always more important than a personal one, but these lines for sure help increase reply rates and email delivery. 

Did I mention that all of these can be made completely automatically using Clay? If you want to see how it's done, send me a DM and I can show you!

### 838. 6971134600249024512

- URL: https://www.linkedin.com/posts/outboundphd_i-dont-see-enough-people-talking-about-one-activity-6971134600249024512-FYG-
- Author: 🦾Eric Nowoslawski (@outboundphd)
- Posted: 2022-09-01T16:00:06.117Z

#### Verbatim Content

I don't see enough people talking about one of the best Sales Navigator filters ever.  
 
It's not current company, current title, or their past school. 
 
It's the fact that you can scan LinkedIn groups! 
 
Why is this one of my favorite filters? Because LinkedIn groups is quite possibly one of the WORST community platforms on the internet. 
 
There's no good way to find the groups organically. 
 
When you're in the group it's full of spam messaging if there is anyone posting at all. 
 
The only way to see the post is if you go to the group and view the posts. 
 
And this is exactly why it's so powerful.  
 
If someone joins a "Marketing Automation" group, I know they LOVE marketing automation. To the point that they searched and found the group on their own one day and have stayed in despite the spammy messages they get from bots.  
 
I use this all the time to find titles with interests that I can't possibly scan for otherwise.  
 
Give it a shot and let me know if you find anything in your niche!

### 839. 6971497003407003648

- URL: https://www.linkedin.com/posts/outboundphd_the-linkedin-gurus-are-going-to-hate-this-activity-6971497003407003648-59RV
- Author: 🦾Eric Nowoslawski (@outboundphd)
- Posted: 2022-09-02T16:00:09.763Z

#### Verbatim Content

The LinkedIn gurus are going to hate this one. I've built a list in Clay  that can replace a ton of basic "ice breaker" Ai companies.  
 
I've used Clay  to take the top 4000 cities in the US and add the top attraction in the city listed on TripAdvisor.  
 
Now you can Index (Match) against your prospect's location and make a P.S. line calling out a landmark in their city.  
 
Please note: this is a spice in your outreach not the whole dang cake. This will not beat relevant messaging and targeting but it does make your prospects wonder if you actually looked them up. 
 
I'd leave the link in the comments but I really don't want the whole world using my gravy train so DM me and I'll send it out to the first 10 people.  
 
P.S. probably shouldn't use this for prospecting into the F1000 or Tech companies. they see this stuff all the time and we need to be thinking a step ahead. But that's for another post.

I've seen far too many people touting advice that are sending only 200 emails a week or have been sending a larger volume for about 2 months. 
 
Email delivery outside of your technical set up (DKIM, SPF, DMARC) can be a black box.  
 
Listen to resources that have been in the game for awhile or have enough data to back up a new "finding."

We were at large tech conference and they had open spots available. 
 
The campaign blew up so quickly I needed to hire two Virtual Assistants to help manage the inbox.

If you can communicate how your product or service helps people, everything else will fall into place.

### 843. 6939259391279845376

- URL: https://www.linkedin.com/posts/outboundphd_growth-growthhacking-leadership-activity-6939259391279845376-Baob
- Author: 🦾Eric Nowoslawski (@outboundphd)
- Posted: 2022-06-05T16:59:24.086Z

#### Verbatim Content

These sites with false senses of urgency kill me…

Ever see crazy claims to artificially increase urgency. I can’t help but eye roll when I see it. 

Some off the top of my head that I’m sure you’ve seen as well could be fixed with just a little bit of extra effort on the fulfillment. 

1) “This is only available for 72 hours!”

Oh really?
 
2) “Only have 2 spots available”

The zoom can’t handle more?

3) “80% off for the first 10 people”

At this point you just made up the price

People see this kind of fake urgency all the time. 

How can we flip all of them to have real urgency

1) Cohorts

Yes there’s only 72 hours because we get started on Monday

2) Include individual attention

Yes only two more spots available because I’ll be helping to implement this alongside them. 

3) Just make a real price

I don’t think further explanation is needed

Let me know if I’m wrong. Certainly open to my mind being changed. 

#growth #growthhacking #leadership

### 845. 6936460736562966528

- URL: https://www.linkedin.com/posts/outboundphd_some-us-take-tech-stacks-way-too-seriously-activity-6936460736562966528-iIoC
- Author: 🦾Eric Nowoslawski (@outboundphd)
- Posted: 2022-05-28T23:38:32.805Z

#### Verbatim Content

Some us take tech stacks way too seriously…

Take cold email for example. There are so many ways that your email can land in spam. Over the last year, I’ve dedicated hundreds of hours to learn and test email deliverability. 

Then I got a great reminder 2 months ago. 

I was at bitcoin conference talking with some companies about their tech stack and such. 

One guy is selling cybersecurity insurance. 

Not warming emails, DMARC wasn’t set up. Sending from one inbox. 

He’s still getting responses with just a first name personalization and congrats on the latest funding round. 

If it’s between getting this stuff perfect and sending emails, just send emails.

Obviously we should strive to perfect any factor in our stack that we can, but if it paralyzes you, just ship it.

### 847. 6936308575766032384

- URL: https://www.linkedin.com/posts/outboundphd_are-you-using-spintax-to-increase-your-email-activity-6936308575766032384-6I3D
- Author: 🦾Eric Nowoslawski (@outboundphd)
- Posted: 2022-05-28T13:33:54.844Z

#### Verbatim Content

Are you using spintax to increase your email delivery? 

There are a ton of factors that come into play when looking at email delivery. 

For example, your email service  provider is definitely looking at how you’re sending the emails. 

Are you sending the same message with a different first name to 200 people at the same time? Your email service provider might start putting you in promotions or worse spam. 

This is only one piece of the delivery puzzle, but one thing you can do is use spintax to increase delivery. 

Spintax is when you tell your email automation tool to use multiple versions of an email to the same list. This way, your email service provider sees a different message every time. 

Most tools do it like this 

Hi, my name is Eric and I love {{outbound|email}}.

That would result in either 

Hi, my name is Eric and I love email.

OR

Hi, my name is Eric and I love outbound.

You can layer on even more spins to get more variations. 

This tool helps you visualize and keep count of how many variations you have 

sp1n(dot)me 

For email pros that already know this, have you ever tried spinning your signature with motivational quotes?

If the message is too short and doesn’t have a lot of good synonyms, I’ll throw 6-10 motivational quotes in my signature to keep my variations over 1000.

### 848. 6937103940983427072

- URL: https://www.linkedin.com/posts/outboundphd_launch-a-cold-email-campaign-for-6-if-activity-6937103940983427072-ouWJ
- Author: 🦾Eric Nowoslawski (@outboundphd)
- Posted: 2022-05-30T18:14:24.689Z

#### Verbatim Content

Launch a cold email campaign for $6...

If I had to start all over again with a tiny budget, here's the cold email tech stack I would use. 

First, we need to define the problems we need to solve. 

For a cold email campaign we need 5 things. 

- An inbox to send from
- A way to warm the inbox
- Data to send to
- Validation to protect our inbox
- Email sequencing

If I missed any, let me know in the comments. 

For the Inbox, let's use Gsuite so that you can use Gmass to warm up the inbox for free. $6

For the email warming, like I said above, GMass so that we can warm for free. 
$0

For the Data, there are tons of websites that have monthly free trials that never end. It'd be hacky, but you'll get there. 

Data providers with excellent free trials

- Apollo.io
- Seamless.AI
- Icy Leads
- Hunter.io
- SalesQL
- Slintel, a 6sense company
- LeadIQ

Let me know of any others in the comments. You can also reach out to Jesse Ouellette or Aditya Prakash who will definitely help you out. 

Now we need to validate contacts. Arguably, this is the most important thing to purchase and if you are getting data from free sources, you should probably invest in this. 

NeverBounce is $4 for 500 verifications. If that still breaks the bank, you can again use GMass for their free verification tool. 

You can use HubSpot, Airtable, Notion for free CRMs

Hubspot even lets you book meetings for free. 

When you're ready to launch a campaign, send it through the Apollo.io free trial. 

Now you have a 25ish email per day campaign going for $6-$10 per month. 

Did I miss anything?

### 849. 6938963726343581696

- URL: https://www.linkedin.com/posts/outboundphd_here-are-8-tips-to-keep-your-deliverability-activity-6938963726343581696-8Scb
- Author: 🦾Eric Nowoslawski (@outboundphd)
- Posted: 2022-06-04T21:24:32.072Z

#### Verbatim Content

Here are 8 tips to keep your deliverability high. 

1. Ensure that you are verifying emails before you send. 

Many think that this is a step in their process that they can overlook but it has to be done every single time. Even if you verified the list 30 days ago, verify again.

2. Ensure you are not using Spam words

Even saying Oprah Winfrey in an email will trigger spam filters. Hubspot has a whole list of spam words to keep out of your email. Some are unavoidable, others you just should never use.

3. Ensure your DKIM, DMARC, and SPF are properly aligned.

Try sending an email through the tool you are going to be using for cold email to Mail genius and make sure your settings are right. 

4. Get a 95 or higher score on Mail Genius

This is an awesome tool to use and make sure your email is set up perfectly and you are not on any spam blacklists. It is my favorite because it gives you suggestions on how to fix your email if there's a problem

5. Make sure you are targeting the right people that you can create a relevant message for

The amount of email has increased tremendously since 2020. Make sure that when you are making the list of people you want to email, have a reason to reach out other than they are the CEO. 

6. Use tools to augment how much research you're doing on your prospects.

Use tools that allow you to mention something specific in your outreach. The best way to know where a company is going in the next 6 months is by checking who they are hiring for. Jordan Crawford has all of this data. 

Think about what pain your customers have, who do they hire to fix it, and what might they mention in a job posting that you can mention in an email?

7. Warm your domains

Warming your inboxes and domains is done by joining a group of other people that want to warm their domains as well and you send messages back and forth to each other which increases the reputation of the inbox. 

8. Keep your email short and to the point.

### 850. 6970047445590274052

- URL: https://www.linkedin.com/posts/outboundphd_here-are-3-ways-you-can-exploit-google-to-activity-6970047445590274052-SLIy
- Author: 🦾Eric Nowoslawski (@outboundphd)
- Posted: 2022-08-29T16:00:08.248Z

#### Verbatim Content

Here are 3 ways you can exploit Google to speed up your prospecting.


Many people don't know that Google is easily your most powerful prospecting tool because they don't know how to use it. 

Google search operators allow you to use Google to cut through the noise and return a search for exactly what you want. 

My top 3 operators are 

-site:
-inurl:
-intext:

Site: tells Google, "give me everything that you can index but only on a specific site. Really useful for finding information about a specific company

inurl: tells Google, "Make sure this keyword is in the url"

intext: tells Google, "Make sure this keyword is mentioned on the page"

So now if you are looking for companies that mention enterprise pricing on their pricing page. 

You can search for your prospect accounts with something like 

site:gong.io inurl:pricing intext:enterprise and if no results show up you know they don't mention it, but if a result shows up, they do. 

Use Clay to score thousands of companies at once with Google Search operators!

### 857. 6941929667759153152

- Author: 🦾Eric Nowoslawski (@outboundphd)

### 858. 6937781604035551232

- URL: https://www.linkedin.com/posts/outboundphd_how-do-you-know-your-email-is-set-up-for-activity-6937781604035551232-FZiA
- Author: 🦾Eric Nowoslawski (@outboundphd)
- Posted: 2022-06-01T15:07:12.151Z

#### Verbatim Content

How do you know your email is set up for cold email or you're on any IP Blacklists?

I'm a nerd about this stuff so I now even check marketing emails from some of my favorite companies and most fail simple steps. 

And this is just for emails that people have opted in to. Imagine what is happening to your cold emails!

Good thing that solutions are pretty simple.

There are three go to softwares that are free that can help with this. 

- Mx Toolbox
- MailGenius
- Mail-tester

All three can point you to what is wrong with your email set up and to fix accordingly. 

My personal favorite is Mail Genius because they offer troubleshooting and solutions to things that aren't set up correctly. 

Make sure when you're running your test you send the email through the tool you plan to send cold emails with or your Email Marketing tool.

### 861. 6935963702705741824

- URL: https://www.linkedin.com/posts/outboundphd_how-can-you-make-a-generic-offer-pop-show-activity-6935963702705741824-ytXh
- Author: 🦾Eric Nowoslawski (@outboundphd)
- Posted: 2022-05-27T14:43:30.699Z

#### Verbatim Content

How can you make a generic offer pop? Show up at the right time...

I was just speaking with a business that at first I didn't think I could help. They resell software that thousands of other consultants are selling. 

When coming in cold, usually this is a tough nut to crack because of how similar the offer sounds on paper. 

However, there are always public pain points that you can track and be the first to talk about in your sales conversation. 

This includes things like...

- Website traffic 
- Social media growth
- Employee headcount growth/attrition
- Employee department growth/attrition
- Job board data (Jordan Crawford has it all)
- What kind of tech is installed in their website
- What activity are they showing up with on LinkedIn
- Does their sales team have Sales Navigator
- Do they have any kind of content led motion
- Did they raise 12 months ago and are probably looking for some dry powder

And the list can go on. 

The best thing we can do is to layer these pain points in outbound motions that not only arm your email sequences, but enable the rest of your sales process.

### 862. 6902626410582941696

- URL: https://www.linkedin.com/posts/outboundphd_you-ever-get-excited-with-a-new-idea-because-activity-6902626410582941696-vcEa

The offer I came up with was 

6 week Weight Loss program
$500 up front and if they lose 20 pounds they get all the money back
Group coaching 3 times a week
Meal plan included
Accountability with cohort, alumni, and expert coaches
Opportunity to upsell supplements

The gym owner was really not thrilled. Any ideas from the crowd of how to make this offer better?

### 863. 6968596086399590400

- Author: 🦾Eric Nowoslawski (@outboundphd)
- Posted: 2022-08-25T15:52:57.252Z

#### Verbatim Content

Asking for support from the LinkedIn community. 

TestBox launched on Product Hunt today and I was wondering if you could help boost the numbers? (link in comments below)

A little about Testbox -- 

The B2B buyer's journey is so broken lately. Buyer's doing their due diligence have to submit their information to get on a demo to even looking at pricing. 

Before they look at pricing, they have to have a meeting with an inbound SDR to qualify them. 

Then they have to schedule another meeting to talk about the features which at the end of the meeting *maybe* they will tell you the pricing or have another meeting to go over it. 

All in an attempt to see if their organization has budget. 

Buyer's deserve a better experience. Leading the charge is Sam Senior at TestBox where they are building a tool that allows buyer's to try software side by side to competitive software before ever talking with a Salesperson. 

Go check them out and their recent product hunt launch linked in the comments below!

### 866. 6904081700201332736

- URL: https://www.linkedin.com/posts/outboundphd_linkedin-doesnt-want-you-know-this-95-activity-6904081700201332736-t1BC
- Author: 🦾Eric Nowoslawski (@outboundphd)
- Posted: 2022-02-28T15:15:48.963Z

#### Verbatim Content

LinkedIn doesn't want you know this. 

95% of databases that provide quality contact information use LinkedIn to source their data. The amount of data that can be connected just by giving a software a LinkedIn URL is amazing. 

So how do you get enough LinkedIn URLs to make it worth your time? Getting them from LinkedIn has limitations and it's pay to play.

I tell everyone, don't get your links from LinkedIn, get them from Google.

There are search parameters that help you get EXACTLY what you want on Google. If you are looking for results that require two inputs like Female Founders. You can modify the search by adding AND to increase the accuracy. 

ex. Female AND Founders

There is a function called site: that allows you to look for results that are only from one site. (I think you know where we are going from here).

search site:www.linkedin.com {{Person Full Name}} {{Company Name}}

99% of the time, the first result on google will be the linkedin URL of the person that you want to contact. 

Bring this process to a Google sheet with some fancy concatenation and combine with a tool to extract the first search result (like botster) and you can untap an infinite amount of LinkedIn urls.

### 869. 6919005213399883776

- URL: https://www.linkedin.com/posts/outboundphd_i-was-just-at-bitcoin-conference-2022-and-activity-6919005213399883776-UupR
- Author: 🦾Eric Nowoslawski (@outboundphd)
- Posted: 2022-04-10T19:36:31.775Z

#### Verbatim Content

I was just at Bitcoin Conference 2022, and was blown away.

By the tech sure, but also by the amount of companies that have no clue how to do sales or marketing. 

I went booth to booth asking about the products and since I'm a Go-to-Market nerd, I got around to asking them about their marketing strategy. 

Many of them admitted to me that they really needed help and I shared with them some strategies I know to work. 

They were ecstatic to hear new creative ideas that have a history of success and we connected in order to talk more at length later. 

I love coming up with new creative ways to market and get people's attention. The truth is that sometimes the basics is all you need. 

From knowing what platforms to be prospecting on, messaging frameworks that resonate with buyers, and follow up methods that move prospects on the fence. 

This stuff is basic to me and I want more people to know about it. 

Would anyone be interested in a 5 day prospecting bootcamp?

Totally free of course as this is just a transfer of knowledge. 

I think I would take on 3-5 people initially to ensure the people that I work with see great results. I want to see who may be interested if I were to put this together.

### 870. 6912119201952317440

- URL: https://www.linkedin.com/posts/outboundphd_gated-has-officially-launching-today-cold-activity-6912119201952317440-1DCd
- Author: 🦾Eric Nowoslawski (@outboundphd)
- Posted: 2022-03-22T19:33:58.710Z

#### Verbatim Content

Gated has officially launching today! Cold email sending is never going to be the same.

A couple months ago, I made a post about how "Winter is Coming" for the cold email senders of the world.

Not just because of the way buyer's are reacting or because of the crackdown on Google and other Email Sending Providers, but the "MOAB" that is Gated

That post led to an amazing conversation with 💌Andy Mowat and myself about the future of cold email.

As a person that manages at least 30,000 cold email sends per week, 💌Andy Mowat and I were completely aligned in vision.

Sellers will now be able to prove the intention behind their email. They will be able to prove to buyers that they are not on some thoughtless email sequence.

Sellers can prove their message was very intentionally sent to a buyer on a silver platter, with a donation to their favorite charity.

For senders, I see this as an absolute win and will in fact save your company time and shorten your sale cycle.

Gated is now available without a waitlist for any Google account user. Get yours today at the link in the comments.

Stay tuned and follow  💌 Melissa Moody 💌Andy Mowat for more updates.

### 871. 6919294729738162176

- URL: https://www.linkedin.com/posts/outboundphd_angel-investors-are-often-overwhelmed-with-activity-6919294729738162176-NGrT
- Author: 🦾Eric Nowoslawski (@outboundphd)
- Posted: 2022-04-11T14:46:57.849Z

#### Verbatim Content

“Angel Investors” are often overwhelmed with the amount of pitching that goes on in their DMs and email. 

Finding the top 10% in the industry you want to enter and asking them to invest is a much better strategy. 

These people 

1) are not being constantly solicited for investment. 
2) have tremendous relevance to your business. 
3) even if they don’t invest, they could connect you to the next person or give valuable advice.

### 873. 6935792343530815488

- URL: https://www.linkedin.com/posts/outboundphd_if-you-could-teach-a-master-class-on-one-activity-6935792343530815488-y6YN
- Author: 🦾Eric Nowoslawski (@outboundphd)
- Posted: 2022-05-27T03:22:35.488Z

#### Verbatim Content

If you could teach a master class on one thing what would it be? 

I was just asked this in a mastermind application I just filled out and immediately, I said Cold email. 

I have spent so much time and practice learning Cold email. Especially for early stage companies, it is the biggest bang for your buck, most scalable channel that they can lean into.

And I know at these early stage companies, every dollar is irreplaceable. I teach cold email as the beginning of everything because this is where you get your message right. This is where you learn if you have product market fit. 

Can you disturb someone's inbox and compel them to positively respond to your offer?

In the inbox, it's you versus them as far as compelling the conversion.

On Google and Facebook there are thousands of reasons why your campaign didn't perform and startups just don't have time or money to figure that out.  

So many people look down on cold email but they don't take note that even Product Led Growth companies understand that it's a great way to target exactly the clients they want and enter them into a conversation.

### 878. 6904450130473537536

- URL: https://www.linkedin.com/posts/outboundphd_sales-b2b-marketing-activity-6904450130473537536-Pwul
- Author: 🦾Eric Nowoslawski (@outboundphd)

#### Verbatim Content

Julia Nimchinski first of all, this line is an absolute banger for LinkedIn...

"The hard thing isn’t dreaming big. The hard thing is waking up in the middle of the night in a cold sweat when the dream turns into a nightmare."

Second, I think we all need to try a myriad of different approaches and measure all of them so that we know what works for us. 

I was just speaking to someone and I had them write down their favorite marketing channels they have used in their business. They said that Video Prospecting yielded them 10 meetings a day. Now they switched to email and it's getting them 1.4...

It doesn't take a McKinsey consultant to say they gotta get back in front of a camera, but they had forgotten just because they didn't monitor and Split test their strategies. 

All of those Levers work and you have to experiment with all of them until you find your lowest Cost to acquire a Customer.

### 879. 6918542133381451778

- URL: https://www.linkedin.com/posts/outboundphd_taking-in-all-of-the-excitement-and-education-activity-6918542133381451778-WH46
- Author: 🦾Eric Nowoslawski (@outboundphd)
- Posted: 2022-04-09T12:56:24.895Z

#### Verbatim Content

Taking in all of the excitement and education at the Bitcoin 2022 conference. Here are the top 5 things I learned. 

1) There is so much funding going into these projects, based on rewards alone, you can get your ticket paid for by downloading apps for a day. 

2) The enthusiasm for bitcoin is so much bigger than I had originally thought. The people here truly see it as the future and the community is incredibly open to new people. 

3) Speaking of being open, the people are extremely friendly and I haven’t seen any gatekeeping. But work needs to be done to bring more women and people form diverse backgrounds into the community. 

From my estimation, the conference is probably 80% men. 

4) Decentralized Finance is definitely the future. So many companies are empowering users to take ownership and control of their own finances. Banks will have a tough time keeping up. 

5) With all the enthusiasm, there are some reminders of the risks. What if the technology does something with a negative externality we haven’t seen yet? What if manhole countries ban it? Can a major country ban it?

Overall, coming to this conference has me even more enthused for bitcoin and web3 in general!

### 881. 6916752639791616001

- URL: https://www.linkedin.com/posts/outboundphd_b2b-sales-growth-activity-6916752639791616001-Qb9X
- Author: 🦾Eric Nowoslawski (@outboundphd)
- Posted: 2022-04-04T14:25:36.376Z

#### Verbatim Content

3 ways to level up your cold outreach today. 

TL;DR: Define your niche, reach out with a trigger backed by data, use an Ai tool like SmartWriter to level up your outreach.

So much of cold outreach feels like spam because your targeting is incorrect, you are reaching out to the wrong people, and you are not personalizing your messaging. 

Targeting

When targeting companies or people for your offer, I find most people are not using a specific enough niche. I think a lot of people are past the "I'll sell to anyone that will buy from me" phase. 

But now, many are still keeping their niche to "Software Tech company that just raised a Series A"

Come on now, we need a lot more targeting finesse than just that. Why not B2B software companies that do not have a VP of Sales in place, have raised anywhere from Pre-Seed-Series A, and do not have a product led growth function. 

Little bit better right?

Now let's talk about who in the organization you should be reaching out to. 

Most of the time, I like to start with the Founder or CEO because the best thing that can happen is to have them delegate me down to the right person. 

Otherwise, if you have customers already, take a look at the roles and responsibilities of your customers. 

What are they responsible for? How is success measured in their job?  How large is their team?

Finally, personalization. For outreach with a contract value between $1,000-$5,000, I like to use 2.5 levels of personalization. (I say 2.5 because the .5 is the person's name. That is more of a requirement than a suggestion)

The other 2 are a trigger event and a personalized line. 

The trigger event is the event, factor, stat, etc. that qualifies this person as a potential buyer for my company. 

Hopefully, you have something much better than a funding round but that still works. This could also include mass hiring, layoffs, product outages, change of pricing, etc. 

I lead the email with this. 

At the end, I use a P.S. line by SmartWriter (Vaibhav Namburi is the founder) to really hit home that this message was meant for that person. My personal favorite is mentioning a restaurant in their area. 

Using even 1 of these will level up your cold outreach for this week. 

Happy prospecting. #b2b #sales #growth

### 889. 6940433451913265152

- URL: https://www.linkedin.com/posts/outboundphd_what-are-you-doing-about-your-email-deliverability-activity-6940433451913265152-Q95O
- Author: 🦾Eric Nowoslawski (@outboundphd)
- Posted: 2022-06-08T22:44:41.954Z

#### Verbatim Content

Email delivery isn’t just a business problem, it’s a human problem. 

The amount of companies I talk to that don’t have their DMARC even set up and they are sharing the marketing domain and the prospecting domain under one roof. 

It’s literally a travesty. 

I don’t mean to be dramatic here but I think the layoffs I’m seeing are hitting me. 

I say it’s a travesty because I immediately think of all the SDRs on PIPs or that have been fired. 

They are given KPIs to hit and expected to hit predominantly through e-mail. But what they are sending from is going to spam or promotions every time. 

And it’s not even their fault. 

That’s what makes me so passionate about giving away what I know about email deliverability. 

It’s much more complicated than just logging into Salesloft and hitting go. 

Appreciate the shout out ⬛️  Eric Steeves™️ 

Cc: Jesse Ouellette Ricky Pearl Amelia Taylor Patrick William Joyce Kris ☁Westcott Aamir Kadri Mauricio Solano 🏴‍☠️⛵🦜

Often times, my favorite way to put together an offer is to look at what the competition will not do, and then let's do that.

For example, Apollo won't let you save more than 25 records at a time. Seamless won't let you save more than 50 records at a time.

We are building a platform that will let you save 100 records at a time. 

Focus on what others won't do, and do that to stand out from the crowd.

### 895. 6893960517527166976

- URL: https://www.linkedin.com/posts/outboundphd_good-marketing-always-puts-people-first-activity-6893960517527166976-a9TZ
- Author: 🦾Eric Nowoslawski (@outboundphd)
- Posted: 2022-01-31T16:57:50.971Z

#### Verbatim Content

Good marketing always puts people first. 

In 2010, no one would have been caught dead without a gated e-book marketing campaign. Today, I believe most companies need to be focused on building engaging communities in service of their Ideal Customer's. 

Businesses typically have to alter to survive in times of extreme upheaval. Many others, on the other hand, prefer to wait for the tide to change before moving. 

A community-driven transformation may invigorate workers and put them ahead of the competition. It's a horrible plan. A corporation may improve itself through communicating to its consumers, channel partners, workers, government, society, and investors. 

Abolition of non-value-adding products and services allows for fresh project development.

Spending is maintained under control by concentrating all efforts on recognizing and satisfying changing community needs. This assists to promote fresh development and future expansion.

 Lou Gerstner revolutionized IBM precisely like this. To break up the massive corporation, Gerstner devised "Operation Bear Hug," which required everyone in the organization to reach out to their most significant clients and find out their greatest issues. 

This discovery led to IBM's rebranding as the "e-business" corporation, today known as IBM.

In tough circumstances, people want to feel part of a community more than before. 

To develop long-term connections with consumers, organizations must create communities that give both practical and emotional value, and have staff and customers collaborate to solve challenges. 

Good marketing always puts people first. Using a group's combined strength is effective marketing in hard times.

### 897. 6893564943770492928

- URL: https://www.linkedin.com/posts/outboundphd_to-my-newly-placed-or-young-professional-activity-6893564943770492928-QdmT
- Author: 🦾Eric Nowoslawski (@outboundphd)
- Posted: 2022-01-30T14:45:58.838Z

#### Verbatim Content

To my newly placed or young professional managers...5 things I wish I focused on as I grew in responsibility in my career

In order to be a successful leader, it is important to establish good management practices right from the start. 

By taking the time to learn and implement the following tips, you can set yourself up for success and help your team achieve their goals.

1. Communicate effectively 

As a new leader, it is crucial to communicate effectively with your team. 

This means sharing information clearly and concisely, listening attentively, and being responsive to questions and concerns. In order to build trust and ensure that everyone is on the same page, regular communication is key.

2. Set clear goals

It is important to set clear and achievable goals for your team. This helps them stay focused and motivated, and makes it easier to track progress.

3. Establish procedures

Establishing procedures gives your team the guidance they need to succeed. 

Even if you think something is obvious, it's worthwhile to take the time to write great guidelines (for example, creating a standard work order or list of tasks). 

It can also help to ask employees what processes they would like established.

4. Create a culture of accountability

When employees are accountable for their actions, you will find it easier to make decisions and respond to challenges. 

If they feel like there aren't consequences for poor performance or mistakes, they may act with impunity. 

Establishing clear guidelines helps your team hold each other responsible without micromanaging or becoming frustrated.

5. Give regular feedback

Regular feedback is key to helping your team improve performance, increasing motivation, and maintaining good morale. 

Employees will feel valued if they can tell you are taking an interest in their careers, accomplishments, and work issues. 

Even informal feedback (such as giving hugs, high-fives or fist bumps) can be appreciated by your team and help to build a strong rapport.

Many may know this but Apple has changed the way that their Mail app receives email and it triggers an open immediately instead of waiting for the recipient to actually open the email, it triggers immediately.

Some data is showing an increase of open rates around 4% since September, however, I have hunch it's much bigger than that.

Keep this in mind if you are doing any email sending #data #email #copywriting

LinkedIn is adding a new feature that I'm very excited for! LinkedIn Audio Rooms are here in Beta! Here's 3 reasons why I'm bullish on Audio Rooms for LinkedIn. (Number 3 is pretty big)

1) The level of trust is so much higher than Clubhouse

Until the onset of Clubhouse, I had no idea millionaires had enough time to spend 6 hours per day on an app. Most of the connections I made on clubhouse claimed they had far more expertise/experience than they actually did. I'm excited for people to have to present through their LinkedIn profiles now.

2) Boosting your brand and getting in front of your audience.

This will be such a great way for people to collaborate brands and instead of posting together, they can easily run an event together.

3) You can invite people to an audio space the same way you can invite people to a regular event...that's right 1000 invitations per week to an audio event. All to your connections and can be filtered to increase relevance. HUGE

### 900. 6888658405976772608

- URL: https://www.linkedin.com/posts/outboundphd_startups-entrepreneurship-business-activity-6888658405976772608-c_5t
- Author: 🦾Eric Nowoslawski (@outboundphd)
- Posted: 2022-01-17T01:49:09.069Z

#### Verbatim Content

How many of us are forgetting the most important rule about selling? Buyers buy on emotion and then justify with logic. 

Rob Turley called me out in a slack channel we both interact in run by Justin Michael (it's over 19k now, you should really join ;)

Julia Nimchinski asked what is the most important tactic when selling on social.

I said providing value of course. Forgetting that the feeling and the emotion that your product or service invokes is far more important than the stated and proven ROI benefits of the product. 

Everyone's brain is like an the combination of an elephant and a rider. The elephant is our emotions and the rider is our logical brain. 

The elephant moves left, the rider claims he wanted to do that all along. The elephant stops, the rider justifies that he needed a break. The elephant turns left, the rider says it is where he needed to go. 

Clubhouse invoked feelings of fear of missing out (FOMO) to galvanize users to join their platform. (remember when people were PAYING for clubhouse invites...)

TexAu (founder Vikesh Tiwari) helps me feel relief knowing that I can automate manual tasks and spend my time in better ways. 

Patrick William Joyce helps me feel more confident that the cold email campaigns I write will actual stick. 

Buyers buy on emotion, and justify with logic.

#startups #entrepreneurship #business #leadership

### 902. 6877287522578284544

- URL: https://www.linkedin.com/posts/outboundphd_leadership-startups-entrepeneurship-activity-6877287522578284544-_VtR
- Author: 🦾Eric Nowoslawski (@outboundphd)
- Posted: 2021-12-16T16:45:19.206Z

#### Verbatim Content

Many first time entrepreneurs are enthusiastic about their idea, but lack the experience necessary to succeed. The three most common mistakes that new entrepreneurs make are not focusing on their customers, spending too much time pitching investors, and they hesitate to build a team. 

These errors can lead to business failure if they are not corrected before it is too late!

-Not Focusing on Customers: Too often, first time entrepreneurs become so wrapped up in their product or service that they forget to focus on their customers. 

It is essential to remember that a business exists to serve its customers, not the other way around! Without customers, there is no business.

Spending Too Much Time Pitching Investors: Another common mistake is spending too much time pitching investors rather than building the business. 

-It is important to remember that investors are not the only ones who can help a business succeed. 

A successful business is built by focusing on customers and product/service development, not by chasing investment dollars.

-Hesitating to Build a Team: One of the biggest mistakes that first time entrepreneurs make is hesitating to build a team. 

Developing the right team will be essential for success, especially if you are an entrepreneur with little business experience or knowledge. #leadership #startups #entrepeneurship

### 906. 6874495855789907968

- URL: https://www.linkedin.com/posts/outboundphd_sales-leadership-business-activity-6874495855789907968-7JyD
- Author: 🦾Eric Nowoslawski (@outboundphd)
- Posted: 2021-12-08T23:52:13.977Z

#### Verbatim Content

Do you know how to extract unlimited searches out LinkedIn?

LinkedIn is the best B2B database in the world. It has phenomenal self reported data that people are incentivized to keep updated. 

The guards at LinkedIn keep everything under wraps though so that you have to pay to play and it's difficult to take advantage of the platform.

They are always on the lookout for malicious scraping, automation, spamming etc. 

Normal accounts can only view 10 results per page at a time. Pay to Play users get a whopping 25 per page. 

LinkedIn URLs are so powerful that with them, many data platforms allow you to unlock so much information. 

So how can you get as many as you want?

Easy. 

Open up Google sheets, concatenate a search to say "site:linkedin.com (Contact First Name) (Company Name)"

Take it over to a tool like Link Prospector and let it extract all of those search URLs for you. 

#sales #leadership #business

### 907. 6875174640394792960

- URL: https://www.linkedin.com/posts/outboundphd_business-leadership-startups-activity-6875174640394792960-1jNO
- Author: 🦾Eric Nowoslawski (@outboundphd)
- Posted: 2021-12-10T20:49:28.838Z

#### Verbatim Content

Are you trying to grow your business or Social following? You may have heard of the $1.80 strategy. Let's talk about what $1.80 looks like in 2022.

Gary Vaynerchuk teaches and used this strategy to build authority and social following. 

The idea is to go into a hash tag of the audience you want to break into and leave an insightful comment (or your 2 cents) on 90 posts a day. Thus adding up to $1.80.

This can still be effective, however, with the rise of what some call Dark Social there are much better strategies now. 

BTW I'm glad you asked. Dark Social includes private groups of people that are congregating on Social platforms privately. Think Facebook Groups, Discord channels, Slack Workspaces, etc. 

These people join these groups to trade ideas, learn, and communicate with people around a specific topic. Often times a topic that builds an audience of your Ideal Customer...

Search out these Dark Social Groups and instead of start speaking in the groups. Ask questions, answer questions, build rapport. Leave your .02 cents all over wherever you can add value. 

It's so much better than mindlessly scrolling a hashtag full of garbage when you could be engaging with someone that needs your product or service right now OR they have a question you could answer. 

Try it out, and let me know what you think. #business #leadership #startups

### 908. 6874090189262733312

- URL: https://www.linkedin.com/posts/outboundphd_leadership-startups-business-activity-6874090189262733312-lkf6
- Author: 🦾Eric Nowoslawski (@outboundphd)
- Posted: 2021-12-07T21:00:15.540Z

#### Verbatim Content

What is the future of Cold Email? 

Today, email senders are dealing with 

More competition
More Spam Blockers
More Filters
More email productivity tech filtering content

According to Mailgun by Sinch, close to 60% of companies have increased their email outreach campaigns since last year. 

It has become a science to get emails to land in inboxes with people needing consultants like Jesse Ouellette or Mark Shalinsky, PhD to have a prayer at hitting inbox. 

Gmail and Outlook are filtering all your content and creating new inboxes for consumers.

And what I see as the M.O.A.B. of all blockers to email marketing, Gated led by aptly titled 💌Andy Mowat (Chief Email Officer)

So what's the answer in the future? 

I think as Sales and Marketers, we are going to have to embrace the future and roll with the punches. 

I don't think the Job Postings for "Email Engineer" are too far off from being posted (it may have already been posted)

I think we are going to have to embrace new forms of communication within emails to boost reply rates. 

Angel Sigman got me with a well placed dog photo.

AND we will have to embrace tech with clear user ROIs like Gated and be ready to either set aside budget to pay the toll or start selecting creative products on Sendoso. 

What do you think? What is the future of email? #leadership #startups #business

### 911. 6867102071355645953

- URL: https://www.linkedin.com/posts/outboundphd_sales-startups-activity-6867102071355645953-OZzY
- Author: 🦾Eric Nowoslawski (@outboundphd)
- Posted: 2021-11-18T14:11:58.398Z

#### Verbatim Content

How much sales tech is too much sales tech?

There are countless amounts of data providers, scrapers, web crawlers, CRMs, document signing platforms, etc. 

At what point is it too much and your data just becomes noise? 

In my high school physics class, we were learning about reducing friction and my teacher asked how much silicon should we use to grease model train tracks. 

She reminded us that you need to put on a perfect amount. Not too much and not too little. Too much and the silicon becomes it's own source of friction. Too little and the object won't overcome the friction. 

It's easy to get lost down the rabbit hole of using too many tools and every company is different. There's no one catch all answer for what is too many tools.

#sales #startups

DKIM, SEP, MQL, ICP, SPF, MVP, ICO, CSR, BDR...acronyms are a barrier in your culture

At 10X Incubator, things move incredibly fast. We have calls with farming data integration tools that are currently selling right into Social platform companies that are in product development right into Blockchain company meetings about fundraising.

Hat Tip to our Chief Product Officer Seth Winters, we are banning all acronym usage. Acronyms make it difficult for new hires to get onboarded and feel included. I know our cofounders feel the same way. 

Also, there could be confusion that no one is asking about and we need to get information transferring across our team.

### 920. 6845121776356233216

- URL: https://www.linkedin.com/posts/outboundphd_gtmgames-sales-marketing-activity-6845121776356233216-Q4vG
- Author: 🦾Eric Nowoslawski (@outboundphd)
- Posted: 2021-09-18T22:30:07.696Z

#### Verbatim Content

Who really drives Revenue? Marketing or Sales?

The age old question is to figure out what is the 20% of activity that is bringing you 80% of your results, and what's the 80% of activity that's giving you nothing. 

Many of us are in the game to sell money at a discount but communicating the message effectively is the difficult question. 

Enter the GTM Games by HYPCCCYCL. An event that has marketers and sellers switch places to test their skills and learn from each other. 

There can only be one winner. Watch to see if I will come out on top. 

Sign up here Hypcccycl.com Justin Michael Julia Nimchinski

To all of my co-founders out there, come watch your VP of Growth show off his Sales chops this week. #gtmgames #sales #marketing #growth #10X #10Xincubator

### 921. 6853691936369348608

- URL: https://www.linkedin.com/posts/outboundphd_linkedin-10x-10xincubator-activity-6853691936369348608-q3o3
- Author: 🦾Eric Nowoslawski (@outboundphd)
- Posted: 2021-10-12T14:04:53.069Z

#### Verbatim Content

How are you formatting your content on LinkedIn?

LinkedIn is an interesting platform because very few people actually post on it. (This is obvious because your newsfeed is still filled with fake activity like the fact that my friends are liking posts.)

LinkedIn rewards users that can post content that retains users on the page. Whatever you can do to stop the scroll will be rewarded by LinkedIn.

Alex Lieberman seems to be taking a twitter approach and using very few characters but staying punchy just like twitter.

Justin Michael is creating long form content that gives away 10% of what he usually shares in his micro-community, "Salesborgs"

Jesse Ouellette is breaking the internet with polls that make you question your beliefs and then you are rewarded with social proof or punished with being outside of the crowd (or vice versa depending on how you look at it)

What other formats for written content are you seeing on LinkedIn and what's the best way to go?

#linkedin #10X #10XIncubator #startups #entrepreneurship #SaaS

Start complaining about your life.

Our very best founders solve for problems they face everyday.

Maybe we can automate a solution, maybe we can connect the missing pieces to create a more enjoyable experience, maybe we can start a tech company together to solve the problem. 

You’ll never know unless you start complaining.

### 937. 6816765517576052736

- URL: https://www.linkedin.com/posts/outboundphd_innovation-ai-tech-activity-6816765517576052736-cOcV
- Author: 🦾Eric Nowoslawski (@outboundphd)
- Posted: 2021-07-02T16:32:28.769Z

#### Verbatim Content

Advancement means opportunities but how do we arrive at that advancement?

Take, for example, the issue of switching everyone to electric cars on our current electrical grid. A recent study showed that in NJ if 50% of people switched to electric cars we would have to double the amount of our current electricity. 

Our grid would not be able to support everyone plugging their cars in at the same time every night. But what if AI was able to control who was charging at what time to be able to stop the grid from overloading?

In the interest of Extreme Ownership, I admit I am no expert in electricity or AI technology, but is there anyone watching this that is and has a solution to this issue? 

Submit Your Tech Idea Here: www.cilapartner.com

#Innovation #AI #Tech #OnAMission #WorldChange #Entrepreneurship

- URL: https://www.linkedin.com/posts/outboundphd_innovation-tech-automate-activity-6815741104072249344-br2v
- Author: 🦾Eric Nowoslawski (@outboundphd)
- Posted: 2021-06-29T20:41:49.557Z

#### Verbatim Content

How would you look at solving a problem?

When I first started working for CILA Labs, I read a book called Ask Your Developer that changed how I looked at tech related problems. The premise of the book was that everything is based off of software and the first thing you should do when you have a problem is to go to your tech developers and ask how they would solve the problem.

This got me thinking- Why shouldn't we automate more of the mundane, more of the little irritations of life?

Do you have a problem that you know would make your life easier if it was automated? 

Submit Your Tech Idea Here: www.cilapartner.com 

#innovation #tech #automate #techideas #entrepreneurship

### 940. 6816463221352894464

- URL: https://www.linkedin.com/posts/outboundphd_entrepreneurship-extremeownership-leadership-activity-6816463221352894464-1fs-
- Author: 🦾Eric Nowoslawski (@outboundphd)
- Posted: 2021-07-01T20:31:15.733Z

#### Verbatim Content

If everything is your fault, everything can be fixed by you.

This is a philosophy I try to live by, the idea of Extreme Ownership (from the book by the same name). By admitting not only what I know, but what I don't know, I can take control because it is always my fault- I did not provide the resources, I did not listen to my experts, etc. Only by admitting my fault can I begin to take back control and work directly with my team to use everyone's expertise to achieve results.

My question for all of you today is what kind of leader do you want to become?

And do you have a tech idea that will help you become that leader?

Submit Your Tech Idea Here: www.cilapartner.com

#Entrepreneurship #ExtremeOwnership #Leadership #Responsibility

### 943. 6816115278498287616

- URL: https://www.linkedin.com/posts/outboundphd_business-people-success-activity-6816115278498287616-WBi_
- Author: 🦾Eric Nowoslawski (@outboundphd)
- Posted: 2021-06-30T21:28:39.692Z

#### Verbatim Content

3 ways to separate your profile from the flood of requests influencers receive. 

Social Media has given people so many amazing ways to connect. I often reach out to my favorite authors on LinkedIn and can tell them how much I enjoyed their book where as this would have had to happen by fate in the past. 

But how do you stand from the crowd? Social Media has made it so easy to connect with people and this ease has made a tidal wave of requests from followers requesting influencers time and energy. 

Here are 3 ways to separate yourself from the crowd when reaching out to people with a large audience. 

1) Follow and engage with them first
It costs nothing to send a DM. Influencer will take notice if they see you giving positive comments on their posts.

2) Keep your message short

Someone with an audience has many requests at all times, keep your request short and to the point.

3) Target people that have deep engagement with the audience you want to attract. 

Just because Logan Paul has 14 million subscribers doesn't mean that it would be worth your time.

### 944. 6815721986006769664

- URL: https://www.linkedin.com/posts/outboundphd_business-people-success-activity-6815721986006769664-ky6v
- Author: 🦾Eric Nowoslawski (@outboundphd)
- Posted: 2021-06-29T19:25:51.455Z

#### Verbatim Content

Who should in your top 100 list? 

Yesterday, I spoke about creating a list of 100 people you would like to meet and sharing it with your established and new network which you can find here 

But who should you put on your list? Elon Musk, Tony Robbins, Dave Portnoy, Warren Buffet?? All of these people have immense connections and status but how would they affect your business? 

Unless you have a way to bring thoughts into the world directly, Elon Musk would probably not add too much to your brand and company. 

When adding people to your top 100 list, think of who is in your industry that is missing what you have or has status and expertise with the market that you want to target.

Thoughtful, targeted engagement is far better than lofty wishful, clout. 

#marketing #business #leadership #startup #entrepreneur #network #cilalabs #teamwork

### 948. 6813924555413827584

- URL: https://www.linkedin.com/posts/outboundphd_blockchain-crypto-tech-activity-6813924555413827584-6Iyv
- Author: 🦾Eric Nowoslawski (@outboundphd)
- Posted: 2021-06-24T20:23:30.607Z

#### Verbatim Content

What is Blockchain?

Technology is not unusual in the sense that it goes through fads of explosion and obscurity. One of the ideas that is getting the most traction is blockchain technology. The problem is there seems to be a misunderstanding of how blockchain technology works. For instance, every crypto is a blockchain, but not every blockchain is a crypto.

Then there is the problem of what needs to be a blockchain — Is it important enough to be kept on a database that every change is seen by everyone? How do we decide what is important enough to keep on a secure database and what can we leave out?

Do you have an idea that needs a blockchain to be secured? 

Submit Your Tech Idea Here: www.cilapartner.com #blockchain #crypto #tech #fintech #innovation

### 965. 6739267683667202048

- URL: https://www.linkedin.com/posts/outboundphd_people-arent-able-to-network-the-same-way-activity-6739267683667202048-mTmg
- Author: 🦾Eric Nowoslawski (@outboundphd)
- Posted: 2020-11-30T20:03:45.325Z

#### Verbatim Content

People aren’t able to network the same way virtually is often seen as a liability. But remember, people aren’t able to network the same way virtually!! 

Do you really think that people are still networking with as much zeal as they used to be before the pandemic? Or do you think people have gotten a little lazier.?

My money is on most people have gotten a little lazier? Yes we can’t network the same way we are used to but take this time now to take digital shots at people you might usually think are out of your reach. 

They aren’t being inundated with requests like they would be after a large presentation they give. 

Keep shooting that shot!!

### 968. 6735307000009265152

- URL: https://www.linkedin.com/posts/outboundphd_business-community-leadership-activity-6735307000009265152-ABoW
- Author: 🦾Eric Nowoslawski (@outboundphd)
- Posted: 2020-11-19T21:45:24.755Z

#### Verbatim Content

The affects of this pandemic on the business community are in a word...unequal. 

Some businesses have no time for my phone call because they are so busy. FedEx has seen numbers that are usually reserved for the Holdiay “busy” season which has started this year in March. 

Other businesses don’t want to hang up the phone because they need so much help from myself and the Chamber. 

I think in this video I could have done a better job conveying I understand the public health situation is dire as well and I firmly believe that we need to be listening to scientists about what needs to be done to control this virus. 

I was just thinking that we have already seen so much damage and with the fear of a second lockdown looking, my heart goes out to all of the struggling entrepreneurs right now. 

If you’re in a business that can benefit from meeting a lot of new people, send me a message. I’m in that business with both feet. 

 #business #community #leadership #newjersey  #success #networking #opportunity #teamwork

### 969. 6734577260021518337

- Author: 🦾Eric Nowoslawski (@outboundphd)
- Posted: 2020-11-17T21:25:41.184Z

#### Verbatim Content

Had a great (PPE filled) time at the Seaman’s Church today with the Port Authority Workforce Development Committee. 

Every year the Seaman’s Church collects thousands of items to donate to our Mariners at sea that can’t be home with their families. 

All items are sorted and put into bags that each member of the crew will receive. Such a great time with the team and great to be doing some meaningful work as well. 

With Hilary McCarron Susan Winfree and many more that I cannot find on LinkedIn at this time 😂😂

Amazon announces they are headed for the Pharmaceutical Industry. Could this be a way for them to protect themselves against a changing Antitrust regulatory environment? I think it could be. And it might just be one of the best decisions they've ever made.

These thoughts are of course my own and if anyone has any other insights, I would love to hear from them. Much to talk about with Amazon and I think this was a phenomenal decision on their part. 

I would love to hear from Radia Carr Robert Di Lauri Andrew Frazier Jr., MBA, CFA Andre Hewitt Jordan Metz Glenn Best and Brian Reilly about this as well. If you aren't connected with any of these people you should reach out!

### 975. 6733090367203233792

- URL: https://www.linkedin.com/posts/outboundphd_networking-entrepreneur-leadership-activity-6733090367203233792-3r1I
- Author: 🦾Eric Nowoslawski (@outboundphd)
- Posted: 2020-11-13T18:57:18.311Z

#### Verbatim Content

Show genuine interest in other people! Like I saw in this video, the best people to network with are Mavens. 

They know who to talk to, what best prices are, and what the best practices are. 

Glenn Best at the Newark Alliance is one of the best Mavens I know. And while we are in groups that have reoccurring meetings, imagine if we hadn’t shown general interest in everyone we meet and we had never partnered. 

That would have surely been a massive loss for the Gateway Regional Chamber of Commerce  and myself personally. 

Show genuine interest in everyone you meet. You’ll never know who that person is or what they do unless you ask. 

 #networking #entrepreneur #leadership #community #business #success #leadershipdevelopment #sustainability
